Hero.badge: Reduce blank time on CSR revisits
Hero.title: Replay the last screen while your CSR app starts.
Hero.subtitle: A three-layer toolkit for visual replay, persistent client cache, and compensating rollback.
Hero.bodyLine1: FirstTx combines Prepaint, Local-First, and Tx into one toolkit.
Hero.bodyLine2: Keep your CSR architecture while adding measured revisit replay, persisted client state, and compensating optimistic UI.

DemoCard.beforeUserRevisitDetail: User clicks back or re-enters the CSR app.
DemoCard.beforeBlankDetail: 1-2 seconds of white screen or spinner while JS and data load.
DemoCard.beforeMountDetail: React mounts only after bundles and API calls finish.
DemoCard.afterBootDetail: A tiny boot script runs right after the HTML is loaded.
DemoCard.afterRestoreDetail: Replays the last DOM snapshot from IndexedDB during revisit boot.
DemoCard.afterHydrationDetail: The React app mounts and data revalidation can run in the background.
DemoCard.viewTransitions: With the View Transition API, restores can become smooth crossfades instead of hard cuts.

HeaderRow.title: Prepaint · Local-First · Tx
HeaderRow.description: Pick the layers you need. Use Prepaint for revisit speed, and add Local-First and Tx for sync and optimistic UI.

prepaint.name: Prepaint
prepaint.description: Capture the last screen as a DOM snapshot and restore it before React loads.
points: Replay on revisit before the main app bundle runs
Reduce the visible CSR boot interval

localFirst.name: Local-First
localFirst.description: Use IndexedDB as a persistent client cache with server revalidation hooks.
points: TTL and staleness metadata built in

tx.name: Tx
tx.description: Run optimistic UI steps with explicit compensating actions.
points: Run a sequence of optimistic steps

Experience.title: User-facing behavior in your app
Experience.description: SSR makes first visits fast. FirstTx focuses on everything that happens after: revisits, back navigation, and tabbing around, so users see a ready screen instead of a blank one.

cards.title: Internal tools with frequent revisits
cards.description: CRM, admin, and dashboard users jump between list and detail all day. Replay the previous visual state during boot instead of starting from a blank view.

cards.title: Refreshing in the middle of a task
cards.description: Local models keep the latest snapshot, so an accidental refresh keeps filters, scroll, and form state instead of starting over.

cards.title: When optimistic UI fails
cards.description: Run optimistic updates as transactions. If the server rejects a change, the screen rolls back cleanly instead of getting stuck half-updated.

cards.title: Handling offline and flaky networks
cards.description: Persist the last client snapshot and revalidate it when the server is reachable again. Application-specific offline writes still need their own policy.

Timeline.title: Sample runtime events (DevTools)

rows.label: tx.commit
rows.detail: UI update and server request succeeded

rows.label: tx.rollback
rows.detail: Network failure → compensate and restore UI state

Timeline.footnote: In the FirstTx DevTools Chrome extension, these events appear as a layered timeline you can filter and inspect.

QuickStart.title: Wire up FirstTx in three small steps
QuickStart.description: Install the packages, enable the Vite plugin, and wrap your root. You can start with all three layers or add Local-First and Tx after Prepaint.
QuickStart.snippetTxTitle: Wrap optimistic updates in a transaction
QuickStart.snippetTxDescription: Update the UI first and sync to the server, with a clear rollback path when requests fail.
